title: Charred Pillowy Ukrainian Garlic Bulb Borscht
description: A kid-friendly twist on traditional Ukrainian borscht featuring a charred garlic bulb and pillowy texture.
instructions: Preheat the sous vide machine to 180°F (82°C).
Submerge the garlic bulb in a sous vide bag with 1 tablespoon olive oil and seal. Cook for 1 hour.
Sear the cooked garlic bulb over an open flame until charred. Remove from heat and let cool. Then, peel and chop the garlic into small pieces.
Peel and shred the beets and onion. Add to a medium saucepan with 2 tablespoons olive oil and cook over medium heat until tender. Add the chopped dill and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cooked beets, onion, and dill mixture to a blender or food processor with 1 cup sour cream, 1 teaspoon sugar, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon black pepper. Blend until smooth.
Add the blended mixture to a large saucepan with 1 cup beef broth, 4 cups water, and the cooked garlic pieces. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to low heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in 1 cup sour cream and 1/4 cup chopped parsley. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Transfer the borscht to individual bowls and garnish with additional parsley, if desired.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
Garnish with a dollop of sour cream and a sprinkle of parsley, if desired.
Store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
